name: ReadMe Developer Hub API
what_it_does: Developer documentation and API reference platform with REST API — creating beautiful interactive API docs from OpenAPI specs, with Metrics dashboard for API usage analytics, Changelog, and developer onboarding guides from a single developer hub.
use_cases: Creating and updating API documentation pages programmatically via ReadMe API
Querying API usage analytics from ReadMe Metrics for developer success tracking
Syncing OpenAPI spec updates to ReadMe documentation via CI/CD pipeline
Managing API version documentation across multiple versions via API
Creating changelog entries and guide pages programmatically from content pipeline
not_for: Internal-only API documentation (use Backstage or Confluence)
Simple static site documentation (use Docusaurus or MkDocs)
Complex multi-product enterprise developer portal (use Apigee or Backstage)
best_when: An API-first company wants beautiful external developer documentation with usage analytics, interactive API explorer, and CI/CD-driven doc updates from a single platform.
avoid_when: You need internal-only docs, simple static sites, or enterprise developer portal with custom identity and complex RBAC.

auth.notes: API key from project settings. Basic auth with API key as username (empty password). Project-scoped access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Webhook for doc version and changelog events.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 3 pages, public docs, basic features
pricing.paid_tiers: Startup from $99/month; Business from $399/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free tier for very basic docs. Per-project pricing. US-based San Francisco company. ReadMe Metrics for API usage analytics is key differentiator. Custom domain on paid plans. SSO on Enterprise. Used by Stripe, Twilio, Plaid for their API docs. 10,000+ developer hubs.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for docs, pages, changelogs, versions, and metrics. Basic auth with API key. Webhook for doc events. Good self-referential documentation (they use ReadMe for ReadMe docs). Project-scoped access. Metrics API requires separate configuration.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Page creation with same slug is idempotent (updates existing). Version management idempotent. Changelog entry creation creates new entries. Metric reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Basic auth with API key as username (empty password) — unusual pattern
Project-specific API key — each project has its own key
ReadMe Metrics requires code instrumentation to collect API usage data
Custom domain requires DNS configuration outside of ReadMe API

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GDPR compliant. US data residency. Basic auth with API key (weaker than Bearer token). No scope granularity. Project-scoped API keys limit blast radius. Standard SaaS security for documentation platform.
